scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the live GitHub docs tree (github.com/r-spacex/SpaceX-API/tree/master/docs, fetched 2026-07-03: capsules/company/cores/crew/dragons/history/landpads/launches/launchpads/payloads/roadster/rockets/ships/starlink, each with all.md/one.md/query.md docs pages -- launches additionally has latest.md/next.md/upcoming.md/past.md and is documented at v5, alongside the legacy v4 route this bundle still uses) and the models/ Mongoose schema source (models/history.js confirms the shared mongoose-id id-stamping plugin applies to every resource, including history, whose docs.md truncated JSON example omits it). The live api.spacexdata.com origin is currently unreachable (Cloudflare 525 SSL-handshake-failed on every endpoint as of this review, consistent with widely-reported reports that the project's origin server is defunct/unmaintained) -- this pass is based on the still-published, versioned API docs and model source, the authoritative documented surface, not a live probe. The API is entirely public/unauthenticated and publishes NO mutation endpoints of any kind: it is a read-only public dataset of historical/reference SpaceX data with no accounts, no user-scoped resources, and no documented write path. All 13 documented GET list resources (the 11 already-covered legacy streams plus 'cores', 'dragons' and 'history', newly added this pass) are now covered_by a stream. Each resource's single-record-by-id GET (/{id}) is excluded as duplicate_of its list stream (the full list already contains every record the by-id lookup would return). launches additionally publishes 4 filtered-view GET aliases (latest, next, upcoming, past) that return a subset/reshaping of the same launches list stream's record population -- all excluded as duplicate_of. Every resource's POST /<resource>/query MongoDB-query-DSL endpoint (arbitrary nested {query, options} filter/sort/pagination body, per docs/queries.md) is excluded as out_of_scope: it is a read operation (not a mutation, so it cannot be modeled as a writes.json action) whose request body is an open-ended Mongoose query object with no fixed field set the dialect's templated stream.query can express, and the plain list stream already returns the full record population the query DSL would filter a subset of -- no read capability is lost by omitting it, only server-side filtering convenience.
